question: How many active users are there?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_users FROM users WHERE is_active = TRUE
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(user_id) FROM users WHERE is_active = TRUE

question: List all products with a price above 500, ordered by price descending.
reference_sql: SELECT product_id, title, price FROM products WHERE price > 500 ORDER BY price DESC
generated_sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.title, p.price FROM products p WHERE p.price > 500 ORDER BY p.price DESC

question: What is the average seller rating?
reference_sql: SELECT AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM sellers
generated_sql: SELECT AVG(rating) FROM sellers

question: How many orders have status 'cancelled'?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cancelled_orders FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(order_id) FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled'

question: Find all categories that have no parent category.
reference_sql: SELECT category_id, name FROM categories WHERE parent_id IS NULL
generated_sql: SELECT name FROM categories WHERE parent_id IS NULL

question: What is the total revenue from all delivered orders?
reference_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'delivered'
generated_sql: SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders o WHERE o.status = 'delivered'

question: For each seller, show their shop name and total number of listed products.
reference_sql:
SELECT s.shop_name, COUNT(p.product_id) AS total_products
FROM sellers s
LEFT JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id AND p.is_listed = TRUE
GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.shop_name
ORDER BY total_products DESC
generated_sql: SELECT s.shop_name, COUNT(p.product_id) AS total_listed FROM sellers s JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id WHERE p.is_listed = TRUE GROUP BY s.shop_name

question: Find the top 5 users by total order spend.
reference_sql:
SELECT u.user_id, u.full_name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
WHERE o.status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY u.user_id, u.full_name
ORDER BY total_spend DESC
LIMIT 5
generated_sql: SELECT u.user_id, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5

question: What is the average review rating per category?
reference_sql:
SELECT c.name AS category_name, AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(r.review_id) AS review_count
FROM categories c
JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id
JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id
GROUP BY c.category_id, c.name
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC
generated_sql: SELECT c.name, AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating FROM categories c JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id GROUP BY c.name

question: For each country, how many users placed at least one order?
reference_sql:
SELECT u.country, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS buyers
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.country
ORDER BY buyers DESC
generated_sql: SELECT u.country, COUNT(o.order_id) AS num_orders FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.country

question: Show the total quantity sold per product, only for products with more than 10 units sold.
reference_sql:
SELECT p.product_id, p.title, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.title
HAVING SUM(oi.quantity) > 10
ORDER BY total_sold DESC
generated_sql: SELECT p.product_id, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id HAVING SUM(oi.quantity) > 10

question: Which sellers have an average product price above 1000?
reference_sql:
SELECT s.seller_id, s.shop_name, AVG(p.price) AS avg_price
FROM sellers s
JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id
GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.shop_name
HAVING AVG(p.price) > 1000
ORDER BY avg_price DESC
generated_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, AVG(p.price) AS avg_price FROM sellers s JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id GROUP BY s.seller_id HAVING AVG(p.price) > 1000

question: Find the total revenue generated by each seller through their products.
reference_sql:
SELECT s.seller_id, s.shop_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS seller_revenue
FROM sellers s
JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.shop_name
ORDER BY seller_revenue DESC
generated_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS total_revenue FROM sellers s JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY s.seller_id

question: List products that have never received a review.
reference_sql:
SELECT p.product_id, p.title
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id
WHERE r.review_id IS NULL
generated_sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.title FROM products p LEFT JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id WHERE r.review_id IS NULL

question: For each seller, find the product with the highest number of reviews.
reference_sql:
WITH review_counts AS (
    SELECT p.product_id, p.seller_id, p.title, COUNT(r.review_id) AS review_count
    FROM products p
    LEFT JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id
    GROUP BY p.product_id, p.seller_id, p.title
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY seller_id ORDER BY review_count DESC) AS rn
    FROM review_counts
)
SELECT seller_id, product_id, title, review_count
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
generated_sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT p.seller_id, p.product_id, COUNT(r.review_id) AS num_reviews, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.seller_id ORDER BY COUNT(r.review_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM products p JOIN reviews r ON p.product_id = r.product_id GROUP BY p.seller_id, p.product_id) SELECT rp.seller_id, rp.product_id FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.row_num = 1

question: Find users who have spent more than the average order value across all users.
reference_sql:
SELECT u.user_id, u.full_name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.user_id, u.full_name
HAVING SUM(o.total_amount) > (
    SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders
)
ORDER BY total_spend DESC
generated_sql: WITH avg_order_value AS (SELECT AVG(total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM orders) SELECT u.user_id, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spent FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id HAVING SUM(o.total_amount) > (SELECT avg_order_value FROM avg_order_value)

question: Show month-over-month revenue growth for the past year.
reference_sql:
WITH monthly_revenue AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month,
        SUM(total_amount) AS revenue
    FROM orders
    WHERE status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)
)
SELECT
    month,
    revenue,
    LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * (revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) /
          NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 0), 2) AS growth_pct
FROM monthly_revenue
ORDER BY month
generated_sql: WITH monthly_revenue AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) AS month, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders o GROUP BY month) SELECT month, revenue, SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS cumulative_revenue FROM monthly_revenue ORDER BY month

question: Find sellers whose total sales have dropped compared to the previous month.
reference_sql:
WITH monthly_sales AS (
    SELECT
        p.seller_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) AS month,
        SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS monthly_revenue
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE o.status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY p.seller_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at)
),
with_prev AS (
    SELECT *,
        LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY seller_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue
    FROM monthly_sales
)
SELECT seller_id, month, monthly_revenue, prev_revenue
FROM with_prev
WHERE monthly_revenue < prev_revenue
ORDER BY seller_id, month
generated_sql: WITH monthly_sales AS (SELECT s.seller_id, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.created_at) AS sales_month, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_sales FROM sellers s JOIN products p ON s.seller_id = p.seller_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY s.seller_id, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.created_at)), lagged_sales AS (SELECT seller_id, sales_month, total_sales, LAG(total_sales) OVER (PARTITION BY seller_id ORDER BY sales_month) AS prev_month_sales FROM monthly_sales) SELECT seller_id FROM lagged_sales WHERE total_sales < prev_month_sales

question: Find the top 3 categories by revenue in each country.
reference_sql:
WITH country_category_revenue AS (
    SELECT
        u.country,
        c.name AS category_name,
        SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id
    WHERE o.status = 'delivered'
    GROUP BY u.country, c.category_id, c.name
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS rnk
    FROM country_category_revenue
)
SELECT country, category_name, revenue, rnk
FROM ranked
WHERE rnk <= 3
ORDER BY country, rnk
generated_sql: WITH ranked_categories AS (SELECT u.country, c.category_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY u.country ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM users u JOIN sellers s ON u.user_id = s.user_id JOIN categories c ON s.seller_id = c.category_id JOIN products p ON c.category_id = p.category_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY u.country, c.category_id) SELECT country, category_id, revenue FROM ranked_categories WHERE row_num <= 3

question: Identify users who placed orders in 3 or more consecutive months.
reference_sql:
WITH user_months AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS order_month
    FROM orders
),
with_gaps AS (
    SELECT *,
        LAG(order_month) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY order_month) AS prev_month
    FROM user_months
),
consecutive AS (
    SELECT user_id,
        SUM(CASE WHEN order_month = prev_month + INTERVAL '1 month' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS consecutive_count
    FROM with_gaps
)
SELECT DISTINCT user_id
FROM consecutive
WHERE consecutive_count >= 2
generated_sql: WITH monthly_orders AS (SELECT u.user_id, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.created_at) AS month, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.created_at) AS year FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id) SELECT mo.user_id FROM monthly_orders mo WHERE mo.month - LAG(mo.month) OVER (PARTITION BY mo.user_id ORDER BY mo.year, mo.month) = 1 AND mo.year - LAG(mo.year) OVER (PARTITION BY mo.user_id ORDER BY mo.year, mo.month) = 0 GROUP BY mo.user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT mo.year) >= 3

                                                                       ^

question: How many customers have completed KYC verification?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS verified_customers FROM customers WHERE kyc_status = 'verified'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(customer_id) FROM customers WHERE kyc_status = 'verified'

question: What is the total balance across all active savings accounts?
reference_sql: SELECT SUM(balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts WHERE account_type = 'savings' AND status = 'active'
generated_sql: SELECT SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a WHERE a.account_type = 'savings' AND a.status = 'active'

question: List all failed transactions in the last 30 days.
reference_sql: SELECT txn_id, from_account, to_account, amount, created_at FROM transactions WHERE status = 'failed' AND created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
generated_sql: SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE status = 'failed' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 day'

question: How many active cards are there per card type?
reference_sql: SELECT card_type, COUNT(*) AS active_cards FROM cards WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY card_type
generated_sql: SELECT c.card_type, COUNT(c.card_id) AS num_cards FROM cards c WHERE c.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.card_type

question: Find customers with a high risk tier.
reference_sql: SELECT customer_id, full_name, email FROM customers WHERE risk_tier = 'high' AND kyc_status = 'verified'
generated_sql: SELECT full_name FROM customers WHERE risk_tier = 'high'

question: What is the average transaction amount by transaction type?
reference_sql: SELECT txn_type, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount, COUNT(*) AS txn_count FROM transactions WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY txn_type ORDER BY avg_amount DESC
generated_sql: SELECT t.txn_type, AVG(t.amount) AS avg_amount FROM transactions t GROUP BY t.txn_type

question: For each customer, show their name and total balance across all their accounts.
reference_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance
FROM customers c
JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id
WHERE a.status = 'active'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name
ORDER BY total_balance DESC
generated_sql: SELECT c.full_name, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id GROUP BY c.full_name

question: Which merchant categories have the highest total card spend?
reference_sql:
SELECT ct.merchant_category, SUM(ct.amount) AS total_spend, COUNT(*) AS txn_count
FROM card_transactions ct
WHERE ct.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY ct.merchant_category
ORDER BY total_spend DESC
LIMIT 10
generated_sql: SELECT ct.merchant_category, SUM(ct.amount) AS total_spend FROM card_transactions ct GROUP BY ct.merchant_category ORDER BY total_spend DESC

question: Find customers who have both a savings and a current account.
reference_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name
FROM customers c
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM accounts a WHERE a.customer_id = c.customer_id AND a.account_type = 'savings' AND a.status = 'active'
)
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM accounts a WHERE a.customer_id = c.customer_id AND a.account_type = 'current' AND a.status = 'active'
)
generated_sql: SELECT c.full_name FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id WHERE a.account_type IN ('savings', 'current') GROUP BY c.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.account_type) = 2

question: Show daily transaction volume and value for the last 7 days.
reference_sql:
SELECT DATE(created_at) AS txn_date, COUNT(*) AS txn_count, SUM(amount) AS total_volume
FROM transactions
WHERE status = 'completed' AND created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY DATE(created_at)
ORDER BY txn_date
generated_sql: SELECT DATE(t.created_at) AS date, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS txn_volume, SUM(t.amount) AS txn_value FROM transactions t GROUP BY date ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 7

question: Find accounts where the total outgoing transfers exceed the total incoming transfers.
reference_sql:
WITH outgoing AS (
    SELECT from_account AS account_id, SUM(amount) AS total_out
    FROM transactions WHERE txn_type = 'transfer' AND status = 'completed'
    GROUP BY from_account
),
incoming AS (
    SELECT to_account AS account_id, SUM(amount) AS total_in
    FROM transactions WHERE txn_type = 'transfer' AND status = 'completed'
    GROUP BY to_account
)
SELECT o.account_id, o.total_out, COALESCE(i.total_in, 0) AS total_in
FROM outgoing o
LEFT JOIN incoming i ON o.account_id = i.account_id
WHERE o.total_out > COALESCE(i.total_in, 0)
ORDER BY (o.total_out - COALESCE(i.total_in, 0)) DESC
generated_sql: WITH account_txns AS (SELECT a.account_id, SUM(CASE WHEN t.txn_type = 'outgoing' THEN t.amount END) AS total_outgoing, SUM(CASE WHEN t.txn_type = 'incoming' THEN t.amount END) AS total_incoming FROM accounts a JOIN transactions t ON a.account_id = t.from_account GROUP BY a.account_id) SELECT account_id FROM account_txns WHERE total_outgoing > total_incoming

question: Find the top spending customer per country based on card transactions.
reference_sql:
WITH customer_spend AS (
    SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, c.country, SUM(ct.amount) AS total_spend
    FROM customers c
    JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id
    JOIN cards cd ON a.account_id = cd.account_id
    JOIN card_transactions ct ON cd.card_id = ct.card_id
    WHERE ct.status = 'completed'
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name, c.country
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS rnk
    FROM customer_spend
)
SELECT customer_id, full_name, country, total_spend
FROM ranked WHERE rnk = 1
ORDER BY country
generated_sql: WITH ranked_customers AS (SELECT c.customer_id, c.country, SUM(ct.amount) AS total_spent, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.country ORDER BY SUM(ct.amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id JOIN cards ca ON a.account_id = ca.account_id JOIN card_transactions ct ON ca.card_id = ct.card_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.country) SELECT customer_id, country, total_spent FROM ranked_customers WHERE row_num = 1

question: Detect customers with more than 3 failed transactions in a single day.
reference_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.full_name, DATE(t.created_at) AS txn_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count
FROM transactions t
JOIN accounts a ON t.from_account = a.account_id
JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'failed'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.full_name, DATE(t.created_at)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3
ORDER BY failed_count DESC
generated_sql: WITH daily_failures AS (SELECT c.customer_id, DATE(t.created_at) AS failure_date, COUNT(t.txn_id) AS num_failures FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id JOIN transactions t ON a.account_id = t.from_account WHERE t.status = 'failed' GROUP BY c.customer_id, DATE(t.created_at)) SELECT customer_id, failure_date, num_failures FROM daily_failures WHERE num_failures > 3

question: How many loan applications are currently pending?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS pending_applications FROM loan_applications WHERE status = 'pending'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(application_id) FROM loan_applications WHERE status = 'pending'

question: What is the average credit score of approved borrowers?
reference_sql:
SELECT AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score
FROM borrowers b
JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id
WHERE la.status IN ('approved', 'disbursed')
generated_sql: SELECT AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id WHERE la.status = 'approved'

question: List all NPA (non-performing) loans with outstanding amount greater than 100000.
reference_sql: SELECT loan_id, disbursed_amount, outstanding, dpd FROM loans WHERE npa_flag = TRUE AND outstanding > 100000 ORDER BY outstanding DESC
generated_sql: SELECT l.loan_id, l.outstanding FROM loans l WHERE l.npa_flag = TRUE AND l.outstanding > 100000

question: How many applications were rejected per loan product?
reference_sql:
SELECT lp.name AS product_name, COUNT(*) AS rejected_count
FROM loan_applications la
JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id
WHERE la.status = 'rejected'
GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name
ORDER BY rejected_count DESC
generated_sql: SELECT lp.name, COUNT(la.application_id) AS num_rejections FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE la.rejection_reason IS NOT NULL GROUP BY lp.name

question: What percentage of repayments were made on time?
reference_sql:
SELECT
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'on_time' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS on_time_pct
FROM repayments
generated_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN r.paid_date = r.due_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(r.repayment_id) AS on_time_percentage FROM repayments r

question: Find borrowers with more than 2 missed repayments.
reference_sql:
SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, COUNT(*) AS missed_count
FROM repayments r
JOIN loans l ON r.loan_id = l.loan_id
JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id
JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id
WHERE r.status = 'missed'
GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name
HAVING COUNT(*) > 2
ORDER BY missed_count DESC
generated_sql: SELECT b.full_name FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE r.paid_amount < r.due_amount GROUP BY b.full_name HAVING COUNT(r.repayment_id) > 2

question: What is the total outstanding loan amount per product type?
reference_sql:
SELECT lp.product_type, SUM(l.outstanding) AS total_outstanding, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count
FROM loans l
JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id
JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id
GROUP BY lp.product_type
ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC
generated_sql: SELECT lp.product_type, SUM(l.outstanding) AS total_outstanding FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id GROUP BY lp.product_type

question: Find the approval rate by employment type.
reference_sql:
SELECT b.employment_type,
    COUNT(*) AS total_applications,
    SUM(CASE WHEN la.status IN ('approved','disbursed') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS approved,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN la.status IN ('approved','disbursed') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS approval_rate_pct
FROM loan_applications la
JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id
GROUP BY b.employment_type
ORDER BY approval_rate_pct DESC
generated_sql: SELECT b.employment_type, COUNT(la.application_id) AS total_applications, SUM(CASE WHEN la.status = 'approved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS approved_applications FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.employment_type

question: For each loan, show the cumulative amount repaid over time.
reference_sql:
SELECT
    r.loan_id,
    r.paid_date,
    r.paid_amount,
    SUM(r.paid_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY r.loan_id ORDER BY r.paid_date) AS cumulative_repaid
FROM repayments r
WHERE r.status IN ('on_time', 'late', 'partial')
ORDER BY r.loan_id, r.paid_date
generated_sql: WITH cumulative_repayments AS (SELECT l.loan_id, r.paid_date, SUM(r.paid_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY l.loan_id ORDER BY r.paid_date) AS cumulative_repayment FROM loans l JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id) SELECT loan_id, paid_date, cumulative_repayment FROM cumulative_repayments ORDER BY loan_id, paid_date

question: Identify borrowers whose credit score is below the average for their city.
reference_sql:
SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.city, b.credit_score,
    AVG(b.credit_score) OVER (PARTITION BY b.city) AS city_avg_score
FROM borrowers b
WHERE b.credit_score < (
    SELECT AVG(b2.credit_score) FROM borrowers b2 WHERE b2.city = b.city
)
ORDER BY b.city, b.credit_score
generated_sql: WITH avg_credit_score AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score FROM borrowers b GROUP BY b.city) SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.city FROM borrowers b JOIN avg_credit_score acs ON b.city = acs.city WHERE b.credit_score < acs.avg_credit_score

question: How many merchants are currently active?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_merchants FROM merchants WHERE active = TRUE
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(merchant_id) FROM merchants WHERE active = TRUE

question: What is the total successful payment volume today?
reference_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_volume FROM payments WHERE status = 'success' AND DATE(paid_at) = CURRENT_DATE
generated_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE status = 'success' AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM CURRENT_DATE) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM paid_at)

question: How many open disputes exist per merchant?
reference_sql:
SELECT p.merchant_id, COUNT(d.dispute_id) AS open_disputes
FROM disputes d
JOIN payments py ON d.payment_id = py.payment_id
JOIN payment_links pl ON py.link_id = pl.link_id
JOIN merchants p ON pl.merchant_id = p.merchant_id
WHERE d.status = 'open'
GROUP BY p.merchant_id
ORDER BY open_disputes DESC
generated_sql: SELECT m.merchant_id, COUNT(d.dispute_id) AS num_open_disputes FROM merchants m JOIN payments p ON m.merchant_id = p.link_id JOIN disputes d ON p.payment_id = d.payment_id WHERE d.status = 'open' GROUP BY m.merchant_id

question: What is the settlement success rate per merchant?
reference_sql:
SELECT merchant_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_settlements,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'processed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS successful,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'processed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS success_rate
FROM settlements
GROUP BY merchant_id
ORDER BY success_rate
generated_sql: WITH settlement_attempts AS (SELECT m.merchant_id, COUNT(DISTINCT s.settlement_id) AS attempts FROM merchants m JOIN settlements s ON m.merchant_id = s.merchant_id GROUP BY m.merchant_id), successful_settlements AS (SELECT m.merchant_id, COUNT(DISTINCT s.settlement_id) AS successes FROM merchants m JOIN settlements s ON m.merchant_id = s.merchant_id WHERE s.status = 'success' GROUP BY m.merchant_id) SELECT sa.merchant_id, sa.attempts, ss.successes, CAST(ss.successes AS DECIMAL(6,2)) / sa.attempts AS success_rate FROM settlement_attempts sa JOIN successful_settlements ss ON sa.merchant_id = ss.merchant_id

question: Find merchants whose dispute rate (disputes/payments) exceeds 5%.
reference_sql:
WITH merchant_stats AS (
    SELECT
        pl.merchant_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT py.payment_id) AS total_payments,
        COUNT(DISTINCT d.dispute_id) AS total_disputes
    FROM payment_links pl
    JOIN payments py ON pl.link_id = py.link_id
    LEFT JOIN disputes d ON py.payment_id = d.payment_id
    WHERE py.status = 'success'
    GROUP BY pl.merchant_id
)
SELECT merchant_id, total_payments, total_disputes,
    ROUND(100.0 * total_disputes / NULLIF(total_payments, 0), 2) AS dispute_rate_pct
FROM merchant_stats
WHERE total_disputes * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_payments, 0) > 5
ORDER BY dispute_rate_pct DESC
generated_sql: WITH dispute_rates AS (SELECT m.merchant_id, m.business_name, COUNT(d.dispute_id) / COUNT(p.payment_id) AS dispute_rate FROM merchants m JOIN payments p ON m.merchant_id = p.link_id JOIN payment_links pl ON p.link_id = pl.link_id JOIN disputes d ON p.payment_id = d.payment_id GROUP BY m.merchant_id, m.business_name) SELECT * FROM dispute_rates WHERE dispute_rate > 0.05

question: Show 7-day rolling average payment volume per merchant.
reference_sql:
WITH daily_volume AS (
    SELECT pl.merchant_id, DATE(py.paid_at) AS pay_date, SUM(py.amount) AS daily_total
    FROM payments py
    JOIN payment_links pl ON py.link_id = pl.link_id
    WHERE py.status = 'success'
    GROUP BY pl.merchant_id, DATE(py.paid_at)
)
SELECT merchant_id, pay_date, daily_total,
    AVG(daily_total) OVER (
        PARTITION BY merchant_id
        ORDER BY pay_date
        ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS rolling_7d_avg
FROM daily_volume
ORDER BY merchant_id, pay_date
generated_sql: WITH daily_payments AS (SELECT m.merchant_id, DATE(p.paid_at) AS date, SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount FROM merchants m JOIN payments p ON m.merchant_id = p.link_id JOIN payment_links pl ON p.link_id = pl.link_id GROUP BY m.merchant_id, DATE(p.paid_at)), rolling_avg AS (SELECT merchant_id, date, AVG(total_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY merchant_id ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS avg_amount FROM daily_payments) SELECT merchant_id, date, avg_amount FROM rolling_avg

question: How many investors have an aggressive risk profile?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS aggressive_investors FROM investors WHERE risk_profile = 'aggressive'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(investor_id) FROM investors WHERE risk_profile = 'aggressive'

question: What is the total current value of all equity holdings?
reference_sql:
SELECT SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_equity_value
FROM holdings h
JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id
WHERE i.instrument_type = 'equity'
generated_sql: SELECT SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_current_value FROM holdings h JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE i.instrument_type = 'Equity'

question: Find the top 5 most traded instruments by number of trades.
reference_sql:
SELECT i.symbol, i.name, COUNT(t.trade_id) AS trade_count
FROM instruments i
JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id
WHERE t.status = 'executed'
GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.symbol, i.name
ORDER BY trade_count DESC
LIMIT 5
generated_sql: SELECT i.symbol, COUNT(t.trade_id) AS num_trades FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id GROUP BY i.symbol ORDER BY num_trades DESC LIMIT 5

question: For each investor, calculate the unrealised P&L across their portfolio.
reference_sql:
SELECT
    inv.investor_id,
    inv.full_name,
    SUM(h.quantity * (h.current_price - h.avg_buy_price)) AS unrealised_pnl
FROM investors inv
JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id
JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id
GROUP BY inv.investor_id, inv.full_name
ORDER BY unrealised_pnl DESC
generated_sql: WITH unrealized_pnl AS (SELECT p.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * (h.current_price - h.avg_buy_price)) AS unrealized_pnl FROM holdings h JOIN portfolios p ON h.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id) SELECT i.full_name, up.unrealized_pnl FROM unrealized_pnl up JOIN investors i ON up.investor_id = i.investor_id

question: Find instruments where the number of sell trades exceeds buy trades in the last 30 days.
reference_sql:
WITH trade_counts AS (
    SELECT instrument_id,
        SUM(CASE WHEN trade_type = 'buy' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS buys,
        SUM(CASE WHEN trade_type = 'sell' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS sells
    FROM trades
    WHERE status = 'executed' AND traded_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
    GROUP BY instrument_id
)
SELECT i.symbol, i.name, tc.buys, tc.sells
FROM trade_counts tc
JOIN instruments i ON tc.instrument_id = i.instrument_id
WHERE tc.sells > tc.buys
ORDER BY (tc.sells - tc.buys) DESC
generated_sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT instrument_id, COUNT(CASE WHEN trade_type = 'buy' THEN 1 END) AS num_buys, COUNT(CASE WHEN trade_type = 'sell' THEN 1 END) AS num_sells FROM trades WHERE traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 day' GROUP BY instrument_id) SELECT i.symbol, i.name, rt.num_sells - rt.num_buys AS delta FROM instruments i JOIN recent_trades rt ON i.instrument_id = rt.instrument_id WHERE rt.num_sells > rt.num_buys

question: Which stores have inventory below reorder level for any product?
reference_sql:
SELECT s.store_id, s.city, p.name AS product_name, i.quantity, i.reorder_level
FROM inventory i
JOIN stores s ON i.store_id = s.store_id
JOIN products p ON i.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE i.quantity < i.reorder_level
ORDER BY s.city, p.name
generated_sql: SELECT s.store_id, s.city FROM stores s JOIN inventory i ON s.store_id = i.store_id WHERE i.quantity < i.reorder_level

question: What is the gross margin percentage per product brand?
reference_sql:
SELECT p.brand,
    SUM(t.quantity * t.sale_price) AS total_revenue,
    SUM(t.quantity * p.cost_price) AS total_cost,
    ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(t.quantity * t.sale_price) - SUM(t.quantity * p.cost_price)) /
          NULLIF(SUM(t.quantity * t.sale_price), 0), 2) AS gross_margin_pct
FROM transactions t
JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.brand
ORDER BY gross_margin_pct DESC
generated_sql: WITH gross_margin AS (SELECT p.brand, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_revenue, SUM(p.cost_price * t.quantity) AS total_cost FROM products p JOIN transactions t ON p.product_id = t.product_id GROUP BY p.brand) SELECT brand, (total_revenue - total_cost) / total_revenue * 100 AS gross_margin_percentage FROM gross_margin

question: Find products whose sales increased week-over-week for 3 consecutive weeks.
reference_sql:
WITH weekly_sales AS (
    SELECT product_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('week', txn_timestamp) AS week,
        SUM(quantity * sale_price) AS weekly_revenue
    FROM transactions
    GROUP BY product_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', txn_timestamp)
),
with_prev AS (
    SELECT *,
        LAG(weekly_revenue, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY week) AS prev1,
        LAG(weekly_revenue, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY week) AS prev2
    FROM weekly_sales
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.product_id, p.name
FROM with_prev wp
JOIN products p ON wp.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE wp.weekly_revenue > wp.prev1 AND wp.prev1 > wp.prev2
generated_sql: WITH weekly_sales AS (SELECT p.product_id, EXTRACT(WEEK FROM t.txn_timestamp) AS week, SUM(t.sale_price * t.quantity) AS total_sales FROM products p JOIN transactions t ON p.product_id = t.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, EXTRACT(WEEK FROM t.txn_timestamp)), running_total AS (SELECT ws.product_id, ws.week, ws.total_sales, LAG(ws.total_sales) OVER (PARTITION BY ws.product_id ORDER BY ws.week) AS prev_week_sales FROM weekly_sales ws) SELECT rt.product_id, rt.week, rt.total_sales, rt.prev_week_sales FROM running_total rt WHERE rt.week > rt.prev_week_sales + 1

question: What is the monthly recurring revenue (MRR) by plan?
reference_sql:
SELECT pl.name AS plan_name, COUNT(s.sub_id) AS active_subs,
    SUM(pl.price_monthly) AS mrr
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN plans pl ON s.plan_id = pl.plan_id
WHERE s.status = 'active'
GROUP BY pl.plan_id, pl.name
ORDER BY mrr DESC

question: Find customers who churned (cancelled) within their first billing cycle.
reference_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, s.start_date, s.end_date,
    (s.end_date - s.start_date) AS days_active
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status = 'cancelled'
AND (s.end_date - s.start_date) <= 30
ORDER BY days_active
generated_sql: WITH churned_customers AS (SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, s.start_date, s.next_billing FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status = 'cancelled' AND s.next_billing < s.start_date + INTERVAL '1 month') SELECT * FROM churned_customers

question: Calculate the customer lifetime value (LTV) from invoice history.
reference_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.email,
    COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices,
    SUM(i.amount) AS total_paid,
    MIN(i.paid_date) AS first_payment,
    MAX(i.paid_date) AS last_payment
FROM customers c
JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id
JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id
WHERE i.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.email
ORDER BY total_paid DESC
generated_sql: WITH customer_invoices AS (SELECT c.customer_id, SUM(i.amount) AS total_amount FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id JOIN invoices i ON s.sub_id = i.sub_id GROUP BY c.customer_id) SELECT c.customer_id, c.email, ci.total_amount / EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE - c.signup_date) AS ltv FROM customers c JOIN customer_invoices ci ON c.customer_id = ci.customer_id

question: What is the average number of delivery attempts per parcel?
reference_sql:
SELECT AVG(attempt_count) AS avg_attempts
FROM (
    SELECT parcel_id, COUNT(*) AS attempt_count
    FROM delivery_attempts
    GROUP BY parcel_id
) sub
generated_sql: SELECT AVG(num_attempts) FROM (SELECT p.parcel_id, COUNT(da.attempt_id) AS num_attempts FROM parcels p JOIN delivery_attempts da ON p.parcel_id = da.parcel_id GROUP BY p.parcel_id) AS subquery

question: Find delivery agents with a success rate below 70%.
reference_sql:
SELECT da.agent_id, da.name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_attempts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN outcome = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS successes,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN outcome = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS success_rate
FROM delivery_attempts dea
JOIN delivery_agents da ON dea.agent_id = da.agent_id
GROUP BY da.agent_id, da.name
HAVING ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN outcome = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) < 70
ORDER BY success_rate
generated_sql: WITH agent_success AS (SELECT agent_id, COUNT(CASE WHEN outcome = 'success' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*) AS success_rate FROM delivery_attempts GROUP BY agent_id) SELECT da.agent_id, da.name, asu.success_rate FROM delivery_agents da JOIN agent_success asu ON da.agent_id = asu.agent_id WHERE asu.success_rate < 0.70

question: Which features are used most by enterprise accounts?
reference_sql:
SELECT f.name AS feature_name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count
FROM feature_usage fu
JOIN features f ON fu.feature_id = f.feature_id
JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id
JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id
WHERE c.status = 'active' AND f.tier = 'enterprise'
GROUP BY f.feature_id, f.name
ORDER BY usage_count DESC
generated_sql: SELECT f.name, COUNT(fu.usage_id) AS usage_count FROM features f JOIN feature_usage fu ON f.feature_id = fu.feature_id JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.industry = 'Enterprise' GROUP BY f.name ORDER BY usage_count DESC

question: Find accounts at risk of churning: active contract ending within 30 days and renewal probability below 0.4.
reference_sql:
SELECT a.account_id, a.company_name, c.end_date, c.mrr, c.renewal_probability
FROM accounts a
JOIN contracts c ON a.account_id = c.account_id
WHERE c.status = 'active'
AND c.end_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '30 days'
AND c.renewal_probability < 0.4
ORDER BY c.mrr DESC

question: Calculate DAU/MAU ratio (stickiness) per account for the last 30 days.
reference_sql:
WITH dau AS (
    SELECT u.account_id, DATE(fu.used_at) AS use_date, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS daily_users
    FROM feature_usage fu
    JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id
    WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
    GROUP BY u.account_id, DATE(fu.used_at)
),
mau AS (
    SELECT u.account_id, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS monthly_users
    FROM feature_usage fu
    JOIN users u ON fu.user_id = u.user_id
    WHERE fu.used_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
    GROUP BY u.account_id
)
SELECT d.account_id, AVG(d.daily_users) AS avg_dau, m.monthly_users AS mau,
    ROUND(AVG(d.daily_users) / NULLIF(m.monthly_users, 0), 3) AS stickiness
FROM dau d
JOIN mau m ON d.account_id = m.account_id
GROUP BY d.account_id, m.monthly_users
ORDER BY stickiness DESC
generated_sql: WITH daily_users AS (SELECT account_id, DATE(u.last_login) AS date, COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) AS dau FROM users u JOIN accounts a ON u.account_id = a.account_id WHERE u.last_login > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 day' GROUP BY account_id, DATE(u.last_login)), monthly_users AS (SELECT account_id, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date) AS month, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) AS year, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS mau FROM daily_users GROUP BY account_id, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date), EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date)) SELECT a.company_name, du.date, du.dau / mu.mau AS stickiness FROM daily_users du JOIN monthly_users mu ON du.account_id = mu.account_id AND du.date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', du.date) + INTERVAL '1 day' JOIN accounts a ON du.account_id = a.account_id

question: What is the claim approval rate by insurance type?
reference_sql:
SELECT ip.type,
    COUNT(cl.claim_id) AS total_claims,
    SUM(CASE WHEN cl.status = 'approved' OR cl.status = 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS approved,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cl.status IN ('approved','paid') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS approval_rate
FROM claims cl
JOIN policies po ON cl.policy_id = po.policy_id
JOIN insurance_products ip ON po.product_id = ip.product_id
GROUP BY ip.type
ORDER BY approval_rate DESC
generated_sql: WITH claim_counts AS (SELECT ip.type, COUNT(c.claim_id) AS total_claims, SUM(CASE WHEN c.status = 'approved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS approved_claims FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ip.type) SELECT type, total_claims, approved_claims, CAST(approved_claims AS DECIMAL(6,2)) / total_claims AS claim_approval_rate FROM claim_counts

question: Find agents who manage policies with a high lapse rate (more than 30% lapsed).
reference_sql:
WITH agent_policies AS (
    SELECT po.policy_id, po.status, ag.agent_id, ag.name
    FROM policies po
    JOIN agents ag ON ag.region = (
        SELECT ph.city FROM policyholders ph WHERE ph.holder_id = po.holder_id
    )
)
SELECT agent_id, name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_policies,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'lapsed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lapsed_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'lapsed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS lapse_rate
FROM agent_policies
GROUP BY agent_id, name
HAVING lapse_rate > 30
ORDER BY lapse_rate DESC
generated_sql: WITH agent_policies AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, SUM(CASE WHEN p.status = 'lapsed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS lapse_rate FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name) SELECT agent_id, name, lapse_rate FROM agent_policies WHERE lapse_rate > 0.3

question: Calculate the loss ratio (total claims settled / total premium collected) per product.
reference_sql:
SELECT ip.name AS product_name,
    SUM(po.premium_annual) AS total_premium,
    SUM(cl.settled_amount) AS total_settled,
    ROUND(SUM(cl.settled_amount) / NULLIF(SUM(po.premium_annual), 0), 4) AS loss_ratio
FROM policies po
JOIN insurance_products ip ON po.product_id = ip.product_id
LEFT JOIN claims cl ON po.policy_id = cl.policy_id AND cl.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name
ORDER BY loss_ratio DESC
generated_sql: WITH claim_amounts AS (SELECT ip.product_id, SUM(c.settled_amount) AS total_claimed FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id), premium_amounts AS (SELECT ip.product_id, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id) SELECT ca.product_id, ca.total_claimed / pa.total_premium AS loss_ratio FROM claim_amounts ca JOIN premium_amounts pa ON ca.product_id = pa.product_id
